alkylation reagent: alkylation with 50 mM iodoacetamide in the dark for 30 min.
alkylation concentration: alkylation with 50 mM iodoacetamide in the dark for 30 min.
cleavage agent: Protein digestion was performed using 1 µg of trypsin (Promega, Madison, WI, USA) at 37 °C for 16 h.
collision energy: fragmented using high-energy collisional dissociation (HCD) with a normalized collision energy of 27%.
instrument: using LC-MS/MS on an Orbitrap Exploris 240 mass spectrometer
ionization type: through a Proxeon nanoelectrospray ion source.
labeling: protein quantification was carried out using the LFQ algorithm implemented in MaxQuant
reduction reagent: 90 mM DTT, 2% SDS, 80 mM Tris-HCl pH 6.8
reduction concentration: 90 mM DTT, 2% SDS, 80 mM Tris-HCl pH 6.8
